title: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers vote to authorize strike as contract negotiations stall
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Coolidge Corner Theatre union workers in Brookline vote to authorize strike amid stalled contract negotiations.
The union was recognized by management in June 2024, with major concerns over job and part-time worker protections.
Coolidge's management states negotiations are ongoing and hopes to finalize a fair, sustainable agreement without a strike.

title: Alex Cora believes this Red Sox player will slug 30 home runs this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora expects outfielder Wilyer Abreu to hit over 30 home runs this season.
FanGraphs' 2026 projections estimate no Red Sox player hitting more than 18 home runs.
Abreu hit 15 home runs as a rookie and 22 last season despite a stint on the injured list.

title: New NFL mock draft has Patriots taking ‘fascinating’ offensive tackle with pick No. 31
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots expected to draft Arizona State offensive tackle Max Iheanachor with the 31st overall pick in 2026 NFL Draft.
Iheanachor, a 6-foot-6, 321-pound tackle, was a Second Team All-Big 12 honoree with Arizona State.
Morgan Moses, the current Patriots tackle, is 35, prompting need for long-term replacement despite high performance.

title: Worcester school officials address racist comments after girls basketball game
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Worcester officials condemned racist comments following a girls basketball game between South High and Andover High on March 6.
Social media posts targeted South High players with racial slurs; Worcester's NAACP and YWCA condemned these actions.
A video from the game went viral, prompting strong reactions and leading to comments being disabled on social media.

title: Rehoboth man allegedly sexually exploited minors, had ‘hundreds’ of illegal images, videos
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Christopher Mota, 29, from Rehoboth, arrested for exploiting minors and possessing illegal images, appears in federal court.
Authorities found child sexual abuse material during a luggage search at Logan Airport; Mota admitted receiving hundreds of images.
Mota communicated with minors via text and apps; estimated to have interacted with 20-25 girls, paying them for images.

title: Jackson Square intersection reopens after burst water main flooded streets
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jackson Square intersection in Jamaica Plain reopened Thursday after flooding from a burst 12-inch water main closed it for two days.
Repair crews worked 48 hours to replace a ruptured 1897 cast iron pipe, restoring traffic at Columbus Ave by Thursday afternoon.
The flooding affected local businesses, including three daycares, which had to evacuate temporarily until repairs were completed.

title: Former Brigham and Women’s doctor facing new rape charges
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Derrick Todd, 52, was indicted on three counts of rape and 17 counts of indecent assault related to 11 patients.
The alleged assaults took place between 2021 and 2023 while Todd worked at Charles River Medical Associates in Framingham.
Todd previously faced rape charges for incidents in 2022 and 2023; over 200 patients have joined a class action suit against him.

title: Getting a vehicle ready for Van Life
author: John Paul
bullets: John Paul suggests installing a second battery in a van for electricity instead of using a generator for Van Life.
A 2017 Volkswagen Passat 1.8-liter engine uses a timing chain, and proper maintenance ensures longevity.
A reader asked about using OBD2 tools on a 1985 Chrysler LeBaron, emphasizing older methods for diagnostics.

title: Charges dropped against former Brockton choral director after alleged victim declines to testify
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Charges against former Brockton choral director Matthew Cunningham were dropped after the alleged victim declined to testify.
Cunningham was arrested in August 2025 for allegedly paying an underage boy for sex; he pleaded not guilty.
Cunningham is no longer employed by Brockton Public Schools and had been on paid administrative leave since his arrest.

title: 18-year-old and 14-year-old charged in daytime armed robbery in Charlestown
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Davonte Jordan, 18, and a 14-year-old boy were charged with armed robbery in Charlestown after allegedly targeting an elderly woman.
The suspects pointed a gun, stole a purse, and fled towards Monument Square; police later found the purse and credit cards nearby.
Jordan pleaded not guilty and is held without bail; police found the victim's credit card during the 14-year-old's booking.
